summary: freertc postinstall rewrites consumer package.json to unpin its own version and re-runs npm install
details: On install, scripts/postinstall-message.mjs reads the consumer's package.json (located via process.env.INIT_CWD) and, for any entry in dependencies/devDependencies/optionalDependencies naming `freertc` whose version is not already 'latest', overwrites the version specifier to 'latest', writes the modified package.json back to disk, and then spawns `npm install` in the host project to apply the change. This silently defeats the installer's version pinning and lockfile discipline: any future publish of freertc — including a potentially compromised one — will be auto-installed on the consumer's machine without further review. The package mutates a file it has no business writing to (the consumer's own package.json), and the change is durable across future installs. This is install-time tampering with installer-controlled state to guarantee future privileged code delivery.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
